items.title: Commit `.vscode/launch.json` (or equivalent) to the repo
items.avoid: Each engineer maintains their own debug profiles.
items.prefer: Shared `launch.json` with profiles for 'Pipeline', 'Pytest current file', 'Attach to container'.
items.why: Onboarding goes from 'fight with config' to 'press F5'; everyone debugs the same way.
items.example: Document any extra env vars / mappings in the README.

items.title: Use logpoints instead of print
items.avoid: Adding `print()` calls and rerunning to see them.
items.why: Same value, none of the source-tree pollution.
items.example: Add 5 logpoints, see the flow, remove them all — no commit pollution.

items.title: Toggle `justMyCode` deliberately
items.avoid: Leaving it at default while investigating a library bug.
items.prefer: `true` for your own code; `false` when you need to step into deps.
items.why: Default keeps the debugger focused; manually disabling it is essential when the bug is across a library boundary.
items.example: Investigating an OpenAI client error → set `justMyCode = false` and step into the client.

items.title: Guard `debugpy.wait_for_client()` with an env var
items.why: Otherwise a deploy with the flag set will hang the pod forever.
items.example: Set `DEBUG_WAIT=1` only in the specific pod / container you want to debug.

items.title: Map remote paths when attaching to containers
items.avoid: Container at `/app/...`, local at `${workspaceFolder}/...`, no mapping → breakpoints never trigger.
items.why: Breakpoints are matched by source path; without the mapping the IDE thinks no source matches.
